steps.title: Map the equation to the situation
steps.content: \(10{,}000 = 2{,}200 + 200t\) represents: total price = down payment + (amount per payment) × (number of payments). The \(2{,}200\) is the one-time down payment; \(200t\) covers the remaining amount through \(t\) monthly payments.

steps.title: Interpret the coefficient 200
steps.content:
In \(200t\), the factor 200 is multiplied by \(t\) (the number of payments), giving the total from monthly payments. Therefore 200 is the dollar amount of each individual monthly payment.
  • A (amount of the down payment): The down payment is \(2{,}200\), not 200 — wrong.
  • B (amount of each fixed monthly payment): 200 is the per-payment amount, since \(200 \times t\) = total from monthly payments. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C (total amount paid after t payments): The total from payments is \(200t\) (the whole product), not just the coefficient 200 — wrong.
  • D (total number of fixed monthly payments): \(t\) represents the number of payments, not 200 — wrong.
